name: Potted Chicken (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
Or two small ones.
1. Boil the chicken lightly until cooked through. Remove the meat from the bones, then skin and bone it.
2. Mince the meat finely. Add salt, pepper, ground cloves, ground allspice, and finely chopped shallot or onion. Stir in the chicken stock.
3. Place clarified butter into a deep casserole dish. Arrange bacon slices evenly across the bottom of the dish. Reserve some bacon for the top layer.
4. Pour the meat mixture over the bacon. Spread to level the surface. Dot the top with additional pieces of clarified butter.
5. Lay the remaining bacon slices on top of the dish and cover with foil and a lid.
6. Place the casserole in a container filled with hot water, ensuring it reaches halfway up the sides of the dish.
7. Bake at 180°C/350°F for about 1 1/2 - 2 hours until thoroughly cooked. Once done, carefully remove from heat and allow to cool completely.
8. When cooled, press down gently with a spoon. Drizzle clarified butter over the top before storing in a cold place until ready to serve.
Serves 8-10.
